canonical_question: What are the best input validation patterns?
aliases: how to validate user input securely

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Pydantic v2 (2023) broke v1 validator API; Zod 3.x stable since 2022; Jakarta Validation 3.1 (2024)

constraints: ALWAYS validate on the server side -- client-side validation is for UX only and is trivially bypassed
Use allowlist (accept known good) over blocklist (reject known bad) -- blocklists are incomplete by definition
Canonicalize and decode input BEFORE validation -- double-encoding bypasses validators
Validate BOTH syntactic correctness (format, type, length) AND semantic correctness (business rules, cross-field consistency)
NEVER use regex as the sole validation mechanism -- combine with type coercion, length limits, and range checks
Pin validation library versions -- breaking changes in Pydantic v1 to v2, Joi v16 to v17 changed APIs

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to prevent XSS specifically (output encoding, CSP)
Need to prevent SQL injection specifically (parameterized queries)

often_confused_with.label: XSS Prevention (output encoding, not input validation)

inputs_needed.question: What programming language or framework are you using?
Where does the input come from?
